Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab is a disease caused by the ascomycete fungus Venturia inaequalis. The disease manifests as dull black or grey-brown lesions on leaves and fruits.
Apple___Apple_scab.solution: Remove fallen leaves. Apply fungicides like captan or myclobutanil.
Apple___Apple_scab.fertilizers: Balanced NPK (10-10-10) to promote general tree health.

Apple___Black_rot.description: Black rot is a disease affecting Apple crops. It can cause significant damage to the yield if not treated promptly.
Apple___Black_rot.solution: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Improve air circulation and avoid overhead watering.
Apple___Black_rot.fertilizers: Avoid excessive nitrogen. Use a potassium-rich fertilizer to boost Apple immunity.
Apple___Black_rot.pesticides: Apply appropriate fungicides or bactericides recommended for Black rot.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: Cedar apple rust is a disease affecting Apple crops. It can cause significant damage to the yield if not treated promptly.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.solution: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Improve air circulation and avoid overhead watering.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.fertilizers: Avoid excessive nitrogen. Use a potassium-rich fertilizer to boost Apple immunity.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.pesticides: Apply appropriate fungicides or bactericides recommended for Cedar apple rust.

Apple___healthy.description: The Apple plant is completely healthy and thriving.
Apple___healthy.solution: Continue your current care routine. Ensure proper watering and sunlight.
Apple___healthy.fertilizers: Use standard organic compost or balanced NPK for Apple.
Apple___healthy.pesticides: None required. Use neem oil periodically as a preventive measure.
